template_metadata.purpose: Plan to migrate eafix-modular repository to unified physical and semantic identity system, update generation and validation scripts.
breaking_changes: Removed PH-08 Archive & Cleanup (deferred to separate follow-on plan)
Added semantic_coverage_pct metric and acceptance gate
Absorbed contracts from numeric_only_identifier_migration.json and stripped_down_id_migration_spec.json

critical_constraint.name: NO_IMPLIED_BEHAVIOR
rules: Every rule, gate, and contract must be expressible as: config entry, executable command, evidence artifact path, pass/fail condition
Items that cannot be expressed this way MUST be marked NOT_AUTOMATABLE and escalated to manual steps
EVERY STEP must have explicit inputs/outputs/invariants defined

steps: Read the entire template to understand the structure
Fill in all sections with project-specific details
Define step-level contracts for EVERY execution step
Generate complete file manifest with read/write permissions for each phase AND step
Generate executable commands for every gate and validation
Define regex patterns for all expected outputs
Create decision ledger entries for all choices made
Output ONE complete plan document following this structure

step_level_contracts.description: Machine-checkable contracts at individual step granularity
features: inputs: explicit file/data requirements with must_exist checks
outputs: guaranteed artifacts with path policies
invariants: things that must remain true during step execution
failure_modes: enumerated error codes with remediation
allowed_paths: glob patterns for permitted access
forbidden_paths: glob patterns for denied access
read_only_paths: paths that cannot be modified
enforcement via git hooks or pre-execution checks

task_analysis.original_request: Analyze all provided project source files and attached artifacts to identify the exact remaining development steps required to deliver a 100% production-ready, fully functional version of this project.

decision_log_entry.rationale: Migration touches more than 30 files and requires contract-first design.

assumptions: The repository root contains all necessary scripts and catalogs for migration.
Existing module_catalog.json and process_step_catalog.json accurately represent the current semantic state.
Only two legacy module aliases exist (O2_OMS and O3_PNL_CLASSIFIER) and are well-documented.
User has necessary permissions to create and modify files, run Python scripts, and move files to archive.
All cross-layer joins by relative_path use normalized forward-slash paths relative to repo root (path normalization contract).
in_scope: Creation of physical identity configuration and scaffolding.
Development of scripts for scanning, assigning IDs, validating physical layer, mapping semantic IDs, updating generators and validators, and archiving superseded artifacts.
Modification of existing generator and validator scripts to emit and enforce numeric identifiers and cross-layer linkage.
Generation of identifier_map.json and associated schema.
Running the full pipeline end-to-end and validating PASS statuses.
out_of_scope: Changes to core business logic or trading strategies within modules.
Refactoring of application code not directly related to identity migration or archiving.
Any operations requiring external network or API access.
definition_of_done: All non-excluded directories contain a .dir_id file with a 20-digit directory_id starting with 2.
All non-excluded files appear in physical_id_registry.csv with a 20-digit file_id starting with 1.
identifier_map.json assigns unique 5-prefix module_ids to all canonical modules and resolves legacy aliases.
generate_three_artifact_catalogs.py outputs module_catalog.json, process_step_catalog.json, and file_registry.csv with numeric IDs only and canonical_symbol metadata.
validate_three_artifact_alignment.py returns physical_status: PASS, alignment_status: PASS, readiness_status: PASS.
No symbolic module_id values remain in any machine key fields across artifacts.
file_registry.csv includes file_scope_class column (11 columns total).
All paths normalized per path normalization contract.

file_manifest.description: Complete list of files read or written during migration.
file_manifest.purpose: Enable change impact analysis, conflict detection, parallel execution safety, and review scoping.

checks: All paths are absolute or relative to repo root
No duplicate entries within same phase or step
Write permissions don't overlap across parallel phases/steps
All files marked for write have corresponding read permission
File scopes respect worktree isolation boundaries
Step-level scopes are subsets of phase-level scopes

phase_overview.name: Baseline Scan
phase_overview.description: Enumerate repo objects and produce baseline inventory.

phase_overview.name: ID Allocation
phase_overview.description: Assign unique physical IDs and create .dir_id files.

phase_overview.name: Physical Validation
phase_overview.description: Validate physical identity coverage and integrity.

phase_overview.name: Semantic Map
phase_overview.description: Create identifier_map.json assigning numeric module IDs and alias resolution.

phase_overview.name: Generator Update
phase_overview.description: Modify generator to use numeric IDs and physical registry.

phase_overview.name: Validator & Schema Update
phase_overview.description: Update schemas and validators to enforce numeric patterns and cross-layer linkage.

phase_overview.name: Regenerate & Validate
phase_overview.description: Generate updated artifacts and validate full alignment.

rules: Each worktree writes ONLY inside its own root
Shared artifacts written only by orchestrator after validation
No direct commits to base_ref; merge via squash-merge
Worktree must pass all validation gates before merge
Step-level file scopes enforced within worktree

merge.command: git checkout {base_ref} && git merge --squash {branch_name} && git commit -m '[{phase}] {summary}'

priority_rules: Phases with more dependents have higher priority
Phases in critical path have higher priority

ground_truth_levels.L1: Schema-valid config files and initial scaffolding
ground_truth_levels.L4: Cross-layer validation with rejection on invalid states
ground_truth_levels.L5: End-to-end acceptance with all gates passing, semantic_coverage_pct >= 85%

metrics.target: 0 duplicates across physical and semantic IDs
Rerun of generator produces byte-identical outputs
95% of migration scripts covered by unit tests
No new IDs allocated on second run
All generated artifacts validate against schemas
>= 85% of non-out_of_scope files have module_id assigned

final_summary.what_was_delivered: A comprehensive migration plan detailing phases, steps, contracts, and validation gates to implement unified physical and semantic identity and update generator and validator scripts.
final_summary.next_steps: Implement each phase in sequence or parallel where allowed, create scripts and configs, run the pipeline in a sandbox, iterate until all gates pass, then apply to production repository.
acceptance_criteria: All non-excluded directories contain a .dir_id file with a 20-digit directory_id starting with 2.
All non-excluded files appear in physical_id_registry.csv (11-column schema) with a 20-digit file_id starting with 1.
identifier_map.json assigns unique 5-prefix module_ids to all 28 canonical modules (27 pipeline + 1 SHARED_LIBS) and resolves legacy aliases.
generate_three_artifact_catalogs.py outputs module_catalog.json, process_step_catalog.json, and file_registry.csv with numeric IDs only and canonical_symbol metadata.
file_registry.csv includes file_scope_class column (11 columns total).
validate_three_artifact_alignment.py returns physical_status: PASS, alignment_status: PASS, readiness_status: PASS.
No symbolic module_id values remain in any machine key fields across artifacts.
All paths in physical_id_registry and file_registry are normalized per path normalization contract.
Parent linkage valid: every parent_directory_id in physical_id_registry exists as a directory_id.

PH-00.purpose: Set up configuration and scaffolding for identity migration.
invariants: No modifications outside id_migration/ and root files

failure_modes.remediation: Confirm working directory and retry

PH-01.purpose: Enumerate all repository objects and classify included/excluded objects.
invariants: Scanner does not modify repository contents
postconditions: Baseline CSV exists with expected columns

failure_modes.remediation: Review scanner logs, fix issues, and rerun

PH-02.purpose: Assign physical IDs to all repo objects and produce physical registry.

failure_modes.remediation: Investigate manifest and counters, ensure no conflicting IDs, and rerun

PH-03.purpose: Validate physical ID coverage and integrity.
invariants: No modifications to filesystem during validation
Every row has exactly one of file_id or directory_id non-empty
Parent linkage validated: every parent_directory_id exists as a directory_id
No orphaned IDs: every .dir_id file appears in registry
postconditions: Physical_status PASS or appropriate error report

failure_modes.remediation: Review report and fix ID allocation issues

PH-04.purpose: Map symbolic module identifiers to numeric identifiers and define schema.
invariants: Unique numeric IDs for 28 canonical modules (27 pipeline + 1 SHARED_LIBS)
Alias mapping is explicit; aliases only in identifier_map.json
postconditions: identifier_map.json created and validated against schema

failure_modes.remediation: Ensure module list completeness and correct alias resolution

PH-05.purpose: Update generation scripts to use numeric module IDs and import physical IDs.
postconditions: Generator loads identifier_map and physical registry

failure_modes.remediation: Review changes and ensure functions updated correctly

PH-06.purpose: Update schemas and validators to enforce numeric patterns and cross-layer linkage.
invariants: Blank module_id allowed only for test, tooling, legacy, out_of_scope

failure_modes.remediation: Review patterns and ensure correct modifications

PH-07.purpose: Run generator, validate outputs, and confirm all statuses PASS.

failure_modes.remediation: Review reports, fix issues, and rerun

invariants: No code files modified outside id_migration/

failure_modes.remediation: Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.
Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.
Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.

failure_modes.remediation: Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.

Scanner produces classification columns: object_scope_class, candidate_semantic_role, path_normalized, is_semantic_candidate, inferred_module_hint

failure_modes.remediation: Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.

failure_modes.remediation: Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.

failure_modes.remediation: Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.

failure_modes.remediation: Check inputs, configuration, and permissions, then retry.

path_normalization_contract.description: All cross-layer joins by relative_path MUST follow these rules.

rules.root_stripping: All paths relative to REPO_ROOT. No leading / or \.
rules.separator_normalization: Convert all path separators to forward slash / for storage and comparison.
rules.case_handling: Preserve original case. Comparisons case-sensitive on Linux, case-insensitive on Windows.
rules.trailing_separators: Strip trailing separators from directory paths.

path_normalization_contract.validation_gate: Physical validator checks that every .dir_id file relative_path matches the normalized path computed from filesystem position.

invariants: Exactly one of file_id or directory_id is non-empty per row
Every parent_directory_id (when non-empty) appears as a directory_id in another row
No duplicate file_id values across all rows
No duplicate directory_id values across all rows
Every row with is_excluded=false has a non-empty ID
object_type=file implies file_id non-empty and directory_id empty
object_type=directory implies directory_id non-empty and file_id empty
physical_id_registry_contract.generation: Registry is derived after allocator runs, never written by allocator directly.

module_owned.meaning: File belongs to one of 28 canonical modules

legacy.meaning: Deprecated or unmaintained (e.g., Directory management system/)

file_registry_contract.blank_module_id_policy: Blank module_id is allowed ONLY for test, tooling, legacy, or out_of_scope files.

shared_libs.scope: Only files under shared/ directory qualify

legacy_aliases.policy: Aliases exist ONLY in identifier_map.json, never as rows in module_catalog.json

(loop).rationale: Step 25 is a control-flow marker for reentry loop-back, not an independent module.

canonical_symbol.description: Former symbolic identifier retained for readability and search

module_symbol.description: Optional copied symbol on process-step records for readability only

forbidden_as_primary_keys: symbolic module_id values such as D2_CALENDAR_SOURCE_ADAPTER
symbolic process references used as join keys
human-readable names used as join keys

required_modifications.title: Load identifier map
required_modifications.action: Add logic to load identifier_map.json before generating outputs

required_modifications.title: Resolve canonical module symbol
required_modifications.action: Normalize all incoming symbolic module references through aliases mapping before lookup

required_modifications.title: Write numeric module IDs into module catalog
required_modifications.action: Set module_id to numeric 5-prefix value and move symbolic identifier into canonical_symbol

required_modifications.title: Write numeric module IDs into process step catalog
required_modifications.action: Set module_id on each step record to numeric module ID and optionally add module_symbol

required_modifications.title: Write numeric module IDs into file registry
required_modifications.action: Replace symbolic module_id output with numeric module_id output

required_modifications.title: Stop deriving identity from symbolic parsing
required_modifications.action: Use explicit metadata from identifier_map.json instead of treating symbolic prefixes as identity source

input_normalization_rules.resolution: map through aliases then modules table

output_rules.rule: never write symbolic values into module_id
never write symbolic values into module_id
never write symbolic values into module_id

required_modifications.title: Validate numeric module IDs
required_modifications.action: Check that every module_catalog.module_id matches ^5\\d{19}$ and is unique

required_modifications.title: Validate numeric process step IDs
required_modifications.action: Check that every process_step_catalog.process_step_id matches ^6\\d{19}$ and is unique

required_modifications.title: Validate numeric process-to-module foreign keys
required_modifications.action: Check that every process_step_catalog.module_id exists in module_catalog.module_id

required_modifications.title: Validate numeric registry-to-module foreign keys
required_modifications.action: Check that every file_registry.module_id exists in module_catalog.module_id

required_modifications.title: Reject symbolic module IDs in machine key fields
required_modifications.action: Fail if any module_id field contains non-numeric symbolic values in canonical artifacts

required_modifications.title: Validate metadata separately
required_modifications.action: Check canonical_symbol uniqueness and non-empty values, but do not use them as join keys

required_modifications.title: Split alignment and readiness
required_modifications.action: Emit separate alignment_status and readiness_status results

allowed_inputs_during_transition: legacy symbolic module references in source material
required_outputs_during_transition: numeric-only module_id values in canonical artifacts

module_catalog: no symbolic value is used as the primary key
process_step_catalog: no process step joins to a symbolic module identifier
file_registry: no registry row uses a symbolic module identifier in module_id
validator: readiness_status PASS requires no blank file_id and no blank module_id

default_config_values.governed_directory_rule: all directories at depth >= 1 that are not excluded

required_repository_files.purpose: Single source of truth for next ID allocation state.
Central configuration for paths, exclusions, target file extensions, and output locations.
Single entrypoint that runs the migration in a fixed order.
must_do: stop on first non-zero exit code
must_not_do: contain duplicate scan logic already owned by child scripts

required_repository_files.purpose: Create .dir_id anchor files for governed directories that do not already have one.
must_do: treat depth >= 1 non-excluded directories as governed
detect existing .dir_id and leave it unchanged
allocate one 20-digit ID per missing governed directory
write one .dir_id file per allocated directory
support plan mode without filesystem mutation

required_repository_files.purpose: Rename target files that do not already contain a valid ID prefix.
must_do: scan recursively for files with configured target extensions
detect existing valid file IDs and skip them
allocate one 20-digit ID per file being renamed
support plan mode without filesystem mutation

required_repository_files.purpose: Validate all directory and file IDs after mutation.
must_do: check every file ID matches required prefix pattern
fail if any governed directory lacks .dir_id after apply

required_repository_files.purpose: Generate a derived filesystem ID registry from the current filesystem state only.
must_do: read current filesystem state after validation succeeds
emit one registry row per governed directory and one registry row per ID-bearing file
record relative_path, object_type, physical_id, source_of_truth, and status

required_repository_files.purpose: Append-only execution log for plan and apply runs.
Derived registry of current physical filesystem identities.

deprecation_map.replacement_item: allocator implementation retained behind COUNTER_STORE.json contract

must_not_exist_in_design.reason: not required for physical ID migration

must_not_exist_in_design.item: manual allocation path as an official execution path
duplicate allocator implementations in multiple packages
registry mutation inside allocator or validator
must_not_exist_in_design.reason: registry must be derived after validation
